title: campanula patula var. rapunculus
intro: Campanula patula var. rapunculus, also known as Rapunzel or small bellflower, is a variety of flowering plant in the bellflower family, native to Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of colds and fever. The health benefits of Campanula patula var. rapunculus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and has also been used in topical applications for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
